items.title: 'Dire' erosion threat in Penang, NGO says stop-gap fixes not enough
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang's coastline is undergoing a severe and accelerating erosion crisis, with beaches shrinking, trees toppling and entire stretches of shoreline disappearing during high tide. Sahabat Alam Malaysia (SAM) president Meenakshi Raman said the situation in key coastal stretches, such as Batu Ferringhi, Teluk Bahang and Tanjung Bungah, was at "very dire" levels, with visible and ongoing loss of beach width compared with a decade ago. Meenakshi warned that the erosion also posed growing risks to public safety and Penang's tourism economy, which is dependent on its coastal image.

items.title: King Charles grasped 'opportunity' on US trip: Palace
items.summary: LONDON: King Charles III's US state visit was a "risk and challenge" that he "grasped in both hands", a palace aide said Sunday as the monarch returned from a widely praised trip. Charles left for the UK from Bermuda Saturday following what a senior royal aide called a "historic" visit to the United States, aimed at healing strained ties between Washington and London. The speech, marking 250 years since American independence, was "high stakes", the aide involved in the visit told reporters.

items.title: Penang coastline faces rapid erosion [WATCH]
items.summary: audreymd@nst.com.my GEORGE TOWN: Penang is facing a mounting coastal crisis that could see parts of its shoreline permanently erased within the next 10 to 20 years if current erosion trends continue unchecked. Malaysia has lost more than 42,500ha of mangrove forests since 2017 due to coastal erosion and human-driven land conversion, stripping away critical natural defences and leaving coastlines more vulnerable to the advancing sea. Tan said two decades ago, Penang's shorelines retained a degree of natural resilience, with monsoon-driven erosion often followed by recovery.

items.title: Trump says US could restart Iran strikes 'if they misbehave'
items.summary: A senior Iranian official said on Saturday that an Iranian proposal so far rejected by Trump would open shipping in the Strait of Hormuz and end the US blockade of Iran while leaving talks on Iran's nuclear programme for later. When asked about Iran's proposal before boarding a flight to Miami at West Palm Beach, Florida, Trump replied: "They told me about the concept of the deal. Iran says it is ready for diplomacy Trump has said repeatedly that Iran can never have a nuclear weapon and said on Friday he was not satisfied with the latest Iranian proposal, while Iran's foreign minister said Teheran was ready for diplomacy if the US changes its approach.

items.title: Cops to meet MAHB after KLIA2 deaths
items.summary: KLIA police chief Assistant Commissioner M. Ravi said the engagement aimed to improve safety at the airport and prevent similar incidents. "Police will hold discussions with MAHB to enhance security measures at KLIA to address this incident soon," he said. Ravi said the latest incident occurred at 9.10pm.

items.title: Morocco king names son to senior defence post
items.summary: RABAT: King Mohammed VI on Morocco on Saturday appointed his son, Crown Prince Moulay El Hassan, to a senior coordinating post in the north African kingdom's military. According to a statement from the royal court, the king – himself chief of the military general staff – had "graciously decided to appoint" the 22-year-old "coordinator of the offices and services of the general staff of the Royal Armed Forces." © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Zahid: Standoff in Negri almost over
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi believes the political standoff in Negri Sembilan is "almost over", despite the state Barisan Nasional doubling down on its stand to withdraw support for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun. "The main issue arose when the adat institutions and state constitution were involved," he said in an exclusive interview during Umno's 80th anniversary convention here. Zahid said the matter would be addressed at a BN-Pakatan Harapan (PH) political bureau meeting next week.

items.title: Kostyuk's lucky shorts steal the show after Madrid triumph
items.summary: MADRID: Marta Kostyuk's maiden WTA 1000 title in Madrid came thanks to her power, poise and a pair of unexpected "Lucky Shorts." Held alongside her team and her two dogs, Kostyuk showed off a piece of black men's underwear, prompting raised eyebrows and plenty of laughter. So we kept it," Kostyuk told Spanish broadcaster Movistar Plus.

items.title: 'Angry Birds'-style video caricaturing Hizbollah draws rebuke
items.summary: BEIRUT: A video published by a Lebanese TV outlet caricaturing Hizbollah's leaders and fighters as characters from the "Angry Birds" mobile phone game drew a rebuke from the group, which called the clip "offensive" on Saturday. On social media, Hizbollah supporters condemned what they considered the ridiculing of leader Naim Qassem, who is also a Shia cleric, with some reacting by sharing images insulting Maronite Patriarch Beshara Rai, the highest Christian authority in Lebanon. Hizbollah said, in a statement, that the video contained "offensive and cheap insults that degrade political discourse to a repulsive level."

items.title: Zahid: BN likely to go solo in GE16
items.summary: BN chairman and Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the coalition was "almost certain" to contest independently, while still acknowledging the practical need for political understanding with other blocs. "We have gone solo before," he said in an exclusive interview for Umno's 80th anniversary celebration. "We contested solo in GE15 (15th general election) and in the general election before that.

items.title: US airlines step up as Spirit winds down
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US air carriers on Saturday mobilised to help passengers and crew members stranded by the overnight shutdown of Spirit Airlines, after the low-cost carrier's last-minute talks with creditors and the White House collapsed. Other carriers — including American Airlines, Delta Air Lines, United Airlines and JetBlue — moved quickly to scoop up Spirit's customers, offering what some dubbed "rescue fares" to those waking up with cancelled itineraries. Carriers also sought to support marooned Spirit staff — and hire them.

items.title: Senior Iranian officer says fresh conflict with US 'likely'
items.summary: TEHERAN: A senior Iranian military officer said on Saturday that renewed fighting with the US was "likely", hours after President Donald Trump said he was not satisfied with an Iranian negotiating proposal. On Saturday, Mohammad Jafar Asadi, a senior figure in the Iranian military's central command, said "a renewed conflict between Iran and the United States is likely." Iran, he said, was "prepared for both paths."

items.title: Israel strikes several parts of south Lebanon, says Hizbollah
items.summary: BEIRUT: Israel carried out strikes across southern Lebanon on Saturday, its military saying it hit dozens of Hizbollah targets. In a statement, the Israeli military said it had struck Hizbollah targets, adding that "approximately 70 military structures and approximately 50 Hizbollah infrastructure sites were dismantled across several areas" on Saturday. Hizbollah claimed several attacks targeting Israeli troops, saying they were in response to ceasefire "violations".

items.title: Americans on a budget mourn loss of low-cost Spirit Airlines
items.summary: NEW YORK: US travelers lamented news of the shutdown of Spirit Airlines on Saturday, saying that the closure of the ultra-low-cost carrier eliminates one of the few air travel options for low-income and working-class Americans. Spirit abruptly cancelled flights overnight, stranding passengers and staff around the US, Caribbean and Latin America, after collapsing under financial pressures that included a sharp rise in fuel costs due to the Iran war. Airlines like Delta and American Airlines were also offering temporarily lower fares to Spirit passengers.

items.title: 17 foreigners held for failing to produce valid travel documents
items.summary: KUCHING: Seventeen foreigners were detained in an operation around the city yesterday. The operation, which involved 28 enforcement officers, also included thorough checks at locations identified as foreign nationals' settlements. "The operation aims to detect and take action against foreign nationals who violate immigration laws," the statement said.

items.title: Umno must acknowledge past mistakes to regain public support, says KJ
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Restoring Umno's image will be an uphill task for the party to recover public support, but the first step is for the party to acknowledge its past mistakes, Khairy Jamaluddin said. The former Umno Youth chief said he agreed with Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani's assessment that the party's image had not fully recovered. Acknowledging the mistakes is just the first step," said Khairy on the sidelines of the Umno Convention held in conjunction with the party's 80th anniversary.

items.title: Myanmar man found dead after fall into lake
items.summary: The victim, identified as Kyaw Zaw Hein, was found at a depth of about 1.5 metres, about three metres from the lake's edge. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said they received an emergency call at 7.31pm before a team from the Seri Kembangan Fire and Rescue Station was dispatched to the scene. Ahmad Mukhlis said the Water Rescue Unit, which was near the location, immediately carried out a search operation using the arch pattern method.

items.title: UAE exit may not tank oil prices
items.summary: THE United Arab Emirates' withdrawal from Opec is widely seen as lessening the clout of the producer group and initiating a race to boost output, ultimately resulting in sharply lower crude oil prices. It may be the case that the UAE decision to leave the Organisation of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (Opec) does substantially weaken the group, and there may ultimately be more crude supplied by the UAE once, or perhaps if, pre-war shipment volume resumes through the Strait of Hormuz. An important fact is that the UAE and Opec's de facto leader Saudi Arabia are the two exporters most able to ramp up production rapidly.

items.title: Sabah records 357 monkey malaria cases, one death
items.summary: SANDAKAN: Sabah has recorded 357 monkey malaria cases and one death in the first four months of this year, with Ranau, Kudat and Tawau identified as the main hotspots, Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said. All health clinics and hospitals are capable of detecting these monkey malaria cases," he said after launching the Wellness Hub here today. Meanwhile, Dzulkefly said the Wellness Hub initiative was an important catalyst in strengthening the country's healthcare ecosystem towards a more comprehensive and proactive approach.

items.title: NST Leader: RM710mil PACE offers relief, but no shield from global recession risks
items.summary: THE RM710 million Progressive Acceleration for Capability and Employment (PACE) economic "shock absorber" could not come at a more seismic economic juncture. PACE is the response to the impasse at the Strait of Hormuz energy chokepoint. In retaliation to the United States-Israel bombings, Iran littered the Hormuz with sea mines, deployed gunboat attacks and missiles against departing oil tankers linked to Iran's foes.

items.title: Elderly couple killed in Teluk Intan crash
items.summary: IPOH: An elderly couple were killed after the car they were travelling in was involved in a two-vehicle collision at Batu 5, Jalan Maharajalela near Teluk Intan this afternoon, leaving three people pinned in the wreckage. Perak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Operations Division assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said in the 2.40pm incident, the local couple, both aged 70, died at the scene after being trapped inside the Toyota Vios they were travelling in. He said the crash involved a Proton Persona and a Toyota Vios, with all three victims pinned in the vehicles when rescuers arrived.

items.title: MACC records 22 statements in RM1.1 billion semiconductor investment probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has recorded 22 statements in its investigation into alleged abuse of power and misappropriation involving a RM1.1 billion investment deal involving the Economy Ministry. MACC senior investigations director Datuk Seri Mohd Hafaz Nazar said the statements included those from ministers and senior officers from several government agencies since the investigation into the case started on Feb 13 last year. He also confirmed that the MACC will call Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli on Monday to assist in the investigation.

items.title: Missing girl often played at site where she was swept away, says mother
items.summary: KAJANG: A young girl feared drowned after falling into a drain and being swept away by strong currents in Bandar Mahkota Cheras yesterday often played at the location where the incident occurred. The victim's mother, Nordina Zulkarnain, 34, said the area, which includes a field and playground, is a popular spot for children living in the apartment, including her daughter, Nur Qaseh Deandra Mohd Qayyum. Nordina said her husband then contacted emergency services to seek assistance from the Fire and Rescue Department.

items.title: Meth user arrested over Toyota Vios theft in Pontian
items.summary: Police said he has multiple criminal and drug-related offences. District police chief Superintendent Hadzrat Hussein Mion Hussain said police received a report of a missing car on 2 May at 1.00am. He said following the report, personnel from the Criminal Investigation Division and Task Force Unit of the Pontian District Police Headquarters, assisted by personnel from Permas Police Station, carried out an operation in Parit Panjang and Parit Mohayat, Ayer Baloi, Pontian.

items.title: KJ praises Zahid for acknowledging 'shortcomings on both sides'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi's speech calling for Umno to move past grudges seems to strike a chord with Khairy Jamaluddin, who was recently readmitted into the party. The former Umno Youth chief said he was moved by the party president's address at the Umno Convention, which reflected his sincerity in welcoming back former leaders and members under the Rumah Bangsa initiative. Khairy also said he agreed with Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani's assessment that Umno's image had not fully recovered.

items.title: Zahid: Political cooperation no longer optional, but Malay interests remain priority
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said today that political cooperation is now essential rather than optional, but stressed that it must be grounded in clear and firm principles. The Federal Constitution must remain the foundation, and the social contract must continue to bind us. “We must approach politics with wisdom and maturity,” he said.

items.title: Khairy: Umno’s image only restored ‘to some extent’, faces uphill battle to regain trust
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Khairy Jamaluddin today said Umno must acknowledge its past mistakes as a party before it can begin rebuilding trust with voters who have turned away from it. He said the party’s image had improved only “to some extent”. Khairy said Umno must recognise its past failings collectively, while also noting what he described as the party’s wider contributions to the country.

items.title: Today's news in pictures - Saturday, May 2, 2026
items.summary: MISSED what happened today? TUMPAT – Grape farmer Wan Yusoff Abdul Rahman ventures into agri-tourism farming at his orchard in Kampung Belukar near Wakaf Bharu. NSTP/WANNA ABDUL RAHIM KUALA LUMPUR – A unique combination of mural art set against the backdrop of the Merdeka 118 Tower in Jalan Sultan.

items.title: 'Zahid asked me to look into one or two things,' says KJ
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Khairy Jamaluddin, who was recently readmitted into Umno, said party president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has asked him to look into specific matters. Khairy, commonly known as KJ, also made it clear that his return to Umno was without a formal position. But certainly no official position, and I asked also for no official position.

items.title: PKR launches AI-driven election system to modernise campaign machinery
items.summary: The party today unveiled the Teras Election System, an integrated AI-driven platform designed to streamline election machinery, voter data management and targeted communications. PKR said that, amid an increasingly challenging political landscape and intensifying preparations for the polls, the party is adopting a proactive approach. The party said Teras represents a foundational contribution to PKR's future, ensuring that organisational strengths remain intact and continue to evolve across generations.

items.title: Khairy: No official Umno role yet, too early to share details
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Khairy Jamaluddin today said he does not currently hold any official position in Umno. He added that it was still too early to disclose details of any responsibilities he may take on. “I have been asked to look into one or two specific matters, but it is still too early to share details as I have not formally begun,” he told reporters when met at the Umno 80th anniversary convention here today.

items.title: MACC confirms Rafizi to be called on Monday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) today confirmed that it will call Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli on Monday to assist in investigations into alleged abuse of power and misappropriation involving a RM1.1 billion investment deal. MACC senior investigations director Datuk Mohd Hafaz Nazar said the former economy minister is expected to have his statement recorded at the Putrajaya MACC headquarters about 10am on May 4. Hafaz said the case is being investigated under Section 23 of the MACC Act 2009.

items.title: Sabah records 357 monkey malaria cases, one death as hotspots emerge
items.summary: SANDAKAN, May 2 — Sabah recorded 357 monkey malaria cases and one death in the first four months of this year, with Ranau, Kudat and Tawau as the main hotspots, said Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad. All health clinics and hospitals are capable of detecting these monkey malaria cases,” he told reporters after launching the Wellness Hub in Sandakan today. Monkey malaria, also known as zoonotic malaria or simian malaria, is transmitted from monkeys to humans via mosquito bites and is typically caused by the parasite Plasmodium knowlesi.

items.title: Woman jumps out of moving car in Klang, cites fear of e-hailing driver watching her
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 2 — A 25-year-old e-hailing passenger jumped from a moving car near Taman Eng Ann, Klang, yesterday morning after becoming fearful and anxious during the journey when she believed the driver was watching her. Klang Utara District Police Chief ACP S. Vijaya Rao said in the incident, the local woman claimed the driver had been watching her through the rear-view mirror without any clear reason, before she opened the door on the right side and leapt out. “The car was driven by a 47-year-old local man, a full-time e-hailing driver, who at the time was carrying two local female passengers.

items.title: Johari Ghani: Umno must accept reality of 26 seats, face internal weaknesses
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani today said the party is in a rebuilding phase and must accept its current political reality, including holding only 26 parliamentary seats and no longer being the ruling party. He said Umno’s long history of political dominance and success must now be followed by honest reflection as the party works to regain its strength. Johari said any political recovery must begin with awareness of the party’s current position, warning that no organisation can move forward without first acknowledging reality.

items.title: Health Ministry sets up task force to tackle doctor shortage, focus on Sabah
items.summary: SANDAKAN, May 2 — The Ministry of Health has established an Interministerial Special Task Force to examine the issue of shortages of doctors and medical specialists, including aspects of talent retention in the national healthcare sector. “I am aware of this and have requested the (ministry) secretary-general and Health director-general to work together with the Ministry of Higher Education, as well as the Public Service Department and the Ministry of Finance to address this issue, particularly in Sabah,” he said. On another matter, he said the ministry will soon submit a counter-proposal regarding operational expenditure cuts by the Ministry of Finance involving the healthcare sector.

items.title: Dr Akmal: No compromise on ‘red lines’ as Negeri Sembilan proves Umno won’t bow to pressure
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh said that the party remains grounded in principle, insisting it will not be swayed by political pressure. “I want to make it clear that Umno is a party founded on principles. Umno is not a party that simply follows the prevailing winds.

items.title: Puteri Umno chief urges stronger support for M40 women, says group often overlooked
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Puteri Umno chief Datuk Nurul Amal Mohd Fauzi today urged the government to strengthen support for young women in the middle-income (M40) group, warning that many remain overlooked despite facing mounting economic and social pressures. “This group is not merely carrying the burden of daily living, but is also balancing responsibilities and expectations,” she said. She also called on the government to move beyond short-term assistance and instead develop a more comprehensive ecosystem that would enable young women to thrive.

items.title: Kota Bharu college offers support to family of teen student murdered in Ketereh, pledges full cooperation in police investigation
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 2 — Kolej Poly-Tech Mara Sdn Bhd (KPTMSB) has expressed sorrow over the death of its 19-year-old student from its campus here who was found in Ketereh, Kelantan with multiple stab wounds yesterday. The college said it is fully cooperating with the police amid the murder investigations. Nurfisya Zulkifly was discovered dead by the roadside near a paddy field in Kampung Simah in Ketereh with 61 stab wounds on May 1, 2026, Kelantan police said.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of afternoon thunderstorms in all states, heavy rain and strong winds in Klang Valley
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — The Meteorological Department has issued a thunderstorm warning covering the Klang Valley and several other states, with heavy rain and strong winds expected until 6pm today. Affected areas include Selangor, Kuala Lumpur, and Putrajaya, as well as multiple regions across Perlis, Kedah, Penang and Perak. The alert also covers Labuan, with thunderstorms expected across all listed areas.

items.title: Kicked, dragged, dunked in water: Four teen boys under police questioning after report of bullying at Tanjung Kling orphanage
items.summary: MELAKA, May 2 — Police detained four male students to assist in the investigation into a bullying case involving a resident at an orphanage in Tanjung Kling, here. Melaka Police Chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said police received a report on April 29 at about 7.11 pm from the victim’s mother, who alleged that her son, a student residing at Rumah Anak Yatim Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah Al Haj Pantai Puteri, had been bullied by senior students known to him. Dzulkhairi said all four suspects were released on police bail after their statements were recorded to assist further investigation.

items.title: Police chase on NKVE ends with Proton Preve ramming BMW in Subang, fleeing driver has criminal and drug records
items.summary: Petaling Jaya police chief ACP Shamsudin Mamat said a search of the suspects’ car also revealed 17 transparent packets containing suspected drugs, weighing around 16 grammes. “The 45-year-old male suspect drove dangerously, entered the Subang Jaya intersection, and crashed into a black BMW at the traffic lights on Persiaran Tujuan in Subang Jaya, before police finally managed to apprehend him at the scene,” Shamsudin said in a statement today. “The male suspect tested positive for methamphetamine.

items.title: Rain Rave festival debate should not be framed in extremes, says Johor DAP vice-chairman
items.summary: KUCHING, May 2 — The debate over the Rain Rave Water Music Festival 2026 should not be reduced to extreme positions and must instead be approached rationally, balancing values, freedom and economic considerations, according to Johor DAP vice-chairman Sheikh Umar Bagharib Ali. In a statement posted on Facebook, Sheikh Umar said Malaysia’s position as a Muslim-majority country with a diverse society requires policy discussions to be handled in a measured manner rather than emotionally. Sheikh Umar also said Malaysia must mature in its understanding of majority rule and minority rights, stressing that while the majority shapes national direction, minority rights must still be protected.

items.title: Sabah electricity bills rising? Energy company says aircon use likeliest reason as tariff unchanged
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 2 — Sabah Electricity has acknowledged growing public concern over higher electricity bills in recent weeks, following claims on social media questioning whether the increase was due to higher tariff rates. He stressed that the increase in electricity bills is largely driven by higher energy consumption rather than any changes in tariff rates. “The rise in electricity bills is influenced by several key factors, including increased usage due to prolonged hot weather, which has led to more frequent use of cooling appliances such as air conditioners,” he said in a statement issued on Saturday after attending the Sabah Electricity Kaamatan Chess Festival 2026 on Friday.

items.title: Immigration swoops on Kuching premises, detains 17 foreigners in crackdown on undocumented workers
items.summary: KUCHING, May 2 — The Immigration Department (JIM) in Sarawak arrested 17 foreigners in an operation conducted around the city yesterday. JIM Sarawak, in a statement, announced that of the total, 16 individuals were Indonesians, while the others were Pakistani nationals. “All those detained were found to have failed to produce valid passports or travel documents during inspections carried out at several premises, including shared houses and rented rooms,” said JIM Sarawak.

items.title: Abang Johari leads Sarawak delegation to UK, Ireland in push for AI, data centres and semiconductor growth
items.summary: KUCHING, May 2 — Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg is leading an official working visit to Manchester and Bristol, United Kingdom (UK), and Dublin, Ireland, starting today until May 5. “In Dublin, Abang Johari is scheduled to visit the K2 Data Centre, while in Manchester, he will participate in the Sarawak Agenda-Townhall programme in addition to attending a dinner and gathering with Sarawakians. “In addition, Abang Johari will also hold an engagement session with representatives of the Manchester Regional Government at the British Muslim Heritage Centre,” said UKAS.

items.title: After withdrawing support, Negeri Sembilan Umno says MB’s fate now with BN, PH leadership
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Negeri Sembilan Umno will defer to party leadership on its next course of action following its withdrawal of support for the menteri besar, including any decision on a potential replacement, its chief Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said today. Jalaluddin said the state chapter would abide by decisions made by Barisan Nasional (BN) and Pakatan Harapan (PH) leadership, including whether the next menteri besar should come from outside Umno. This concerns governance and the institutions of adat and the Negeri Sembilan monarchy,” he said.

items.title: Jealous rage likely motive behind Ketereh college girl’s killing, Kelantan police say amid week-long remand for boyfriend and buddy
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 2 — Jealousy is believed to be the motive behind the murder of a 19-year-old college student whose body was found with 61 stab wounds in Keterah yesterday. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said investigators suspect the victim was stabbed to death by her 19-year-old boyfriend, who allegedly believed she was having an affair, the New Straits Times reported today. He said the boyfriend, identified as the main suspect, was arrested yesterday alongside another youth believed to be a friend of the victim.

items.title: One day, two fatal falls at KLIA2: Police identify second foreigner as 30-year-old Algerian man
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 2 — Police have confirmed another fatal incident of a foreigner after falling at Kuala Lumpur International Airport Terminal 2 (KLIA T2), last night. KLIA district police chief ACP M. Ravi said in the incident at about 9.10 pm, a 30-year-old Algerian man is believed to have fallen from the outside of the balcony at the terminal. Yesterday afternoon, police reported that a woman from China fell to her death from Level 3 of Kuala Lumpur International Airport Terminal 2.

items.title: Regular play spot but not her slippers, says mum of missing nine-year-old girl swept into Bandar Mahkota Cheras drain
items.summary: KAJANG, May 2 — A nine-year-old girl, feared drowned after falling into a drain and swept away by strong currents in Bandar Mahkota Cheras yesterday, often played in the area where the incident occurred. Day Two of a search and rescue for a nine-year-old girl who was swept away by strong currents in Bandar Mahkota Cheras, Selangor on May 1, 2026. A search and rescue for a nine-year-old girl who was swept away by strong currents in Bandar Mahkota Cheras, Selangor on May 1, 2026, expands into the underground drainage system on Day Two.— Bernama pic Media reports yesterday cited that a nine-year-old girl fell into a drain while chasing a ball, before being swept away by strong currents.

items.title: DBKL imposes 300m buffer zone on Bukit Kiara project after TTDI residents’ concerns, says Hannah Yeoh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has imposed a 300m buffer zone on a proposed development opposite the Taman Persekutuan Bukit Kiara recreational park, following complaints from residents in Taman Tun Dr Ismail (TTDI). “Kuala Lumpur is a city, and its people need green spaces for recreational activities and to de-stress,” she said. Yeoh spoke to the media after an event at the park today, organised by local non-profit Friends of Bukit Kiara (FoBK), where Kuala Lumpur mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud and FoBK president Leon Koay were also present.

items.title: Funeral in Kuala Kangsar for murdered college student, 19; grieving family asks for privacy
items.summary: KUALA KANGSAR, May 2 — The family of a 19-year-old student found stabbed to death 61 times in Kampung Simah, Ketereh, Kelantan, asks for space to grieve in peace. A visit to the victim’s family home in Kampung Batu Jarau here revealed a solemn silence, with only her closest relatives present. She was buried at 5.30am May 2 at Kampung Periang Muslim Cemetery in Kuala Kangsar, Perak.

items.title: Second-day search underway for nine-year-old feared swept away in Bandar Mahkota Cheras drain
items.summary: KAJANG, May 2 — The search and rescue (SAR) operation for a nine-year-old girl feared swept away after falling into a drain in Bandar Mahkota Cheras while retrieving a ball has entered its second day, with search efforts expanded to a five-kilometre radius. According to Sinar Harian , Selangor Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) operations assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the operation involves firefighters from the Kajang station, supported by two Water Rescue Team (PPDA) units from Shah Alam and KLIA. Ahmad Mukhlis said the search team is also using IWK’s pole camera to assist in scanning inside the sewer system.

items.title: Umno must reinvent itself or risk irrelevance, says Mohamad Hasan as party marks 80th anniversary
items.summary: Speaking at Umno’s 80th anniversary gathering — which coincided with his 70th birthday — the Rembau MP said the party could no longer rely on legacy or nostalgia to stay relevant. Some even say they no longer need Umno,” he said. “However, Umno is not dead.

items.title: Pahang man, 65, dies in Mecca after sudden collapse before Friday prayers at Masjidil Haram
items.summary: MECCA, May 2 — A Malaysian haj pilgrim died after collapsing while on his way to perform Friday prayers at the courtyard of the Grand Mosque here yesterday. Malaysian Haj Delegation head Mohd Hisham Harun said the 65-year-old male pilgrim from Pahang was pronounced dead at 12.15 pm local time (5.15 pm Malaysian time) due to heart complications. Mohd Hisham said the deceased, who had travelled to the Holy Land with his wife, was at the Grand Mosque courtyard to perform Friday prayers when he suddenly collapsed and lost consciousness.

items.title: Umno sees membership surge as online registration dubbed ‘gamechanger’, says Asyraf Wajdi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Umno secretary-general Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki today said the party has recorded an extraordinary surge in new memberships, crediting a “gamechanger” move by the party president for enabling online registration. Asyraf said that in a break from tradition, this year’s party celebration is not an exclusive event. The breakdown showed Umno Youth recording 8,233 new members, followed by 1,260 from Wanita Umno and 1,240 from Puteri Umno.

items.title: Ketereh murder probe widens: Four suspects now held over college student found with 61 stab wounds
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 2 — Police have detained two more suspects to help with the probe into the discovery of a 19-year-old woman’s body with 61 stab wounds near paddy fields in Kampung Simah, Ketereh, early yesterday morning. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said a team from the Kelantan Contingent Police Headquarters’ Criminal Investigation Department, working with Kota Bharu district police headquarters, launched a raid from 4.40 pm yesterday until 2.30 am today. He said the raid targeted two separate locations, Taman Murni on Jalan Abdul Kadir Adabi and Kampung Belukar Luas in Ketereh.

items.title: KL must stay open and modern, not be held back by ‘reckless narratives’, says Kepong MP on Rain Rave festival
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — The Rain Rave Water Music Festival 2026 is a lawful and well-organised event that has generated real economic activity for local traders, workers and businesses, said Kepong MP Lim Lip Eng. In a statement today, Lim said the event had attracted large crowds and contributed positively to local economic activity, but noted that some parties were attempting to “manufacture controversy out of nothing”. He said such “empty politics” would only damage public confidence and undermine efforts to grow Malaysia’s tourism sector.

items.title: Songkran is cultural heritage, not water festival entertainment, says Malaysian Siam Association amid growing confusion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Persatuan Siam Malaysia (PSM) president Sam Maark Suvanphet yesterday said that there is confusion between the Songkran celebration and water-based entertainment events such as water festivals or water music festivals. He said Songkran is the Siamese New Year celebration, which carries cultural, family and religious values, including traditional practices such as seeking blessings from parents and performing religious activities at temples. In contrast, he said water music festivals and similar events held at certain locations are entertainment and economic programmes with no cultural or religious link to the Siamese community.
